raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing a complaint regarding unauthorized recurring charges from XXXX, a pettech subscription service. Beginning on XX/XX/year>, I attempted to cancel my subscription directly with the company multiple times. Each time I requested cancellation, XXXX refused to process it and instead tried to retain me by offering discounts or alternative services. They did not honor my explicit cancellation requests, and they continued billing me without my consent. 
XXXX customer platform is nonsensical and does not provide any XXXX XXXX to cancel the subscription. There is no clear cancellation button, no accessible account management option, and no straightforward method to terminate service. The only way to attempt cancellation is through customer support, which repeatedly ignored my requests and used retention tactics instead of processing the cancellation. This means the merchant effectively prevents consumers from canceling, even when they explicitly ask to stop service. 
Because the merchant would not cancel the subscription, I notified American Express that these charges were unauthorized and asked them to stop future transactions. Despite this notice, charges from XXXX continued from XX/XX/year> through XX/XX/year> and beyond. I made it clear that I had withdrawn consent and that the merchant was obstructing cancellation, yet the billing continued. 
This is a merchantfault situation involving : A nonfunctional, confusing customer platform No accessible or legitimate cancellation mechanism Refusal to honor explicit cancellation requests Continued billing after consent was withdrawn Use of retention tactics instead of processing cancellation It is also a cardissuer issue, because Amex continued allowing charges even after I notified them that the billing was unauthorized and that the merchant was preventing cancellation. 
I am requesting the CFPB review both the merchants practices and the card issuers handling of my dispute, as the ongoing charges were not authorized and occurred after I took all reasonable steps to cancel.
